name: Hors
description: Hors is a Slavic deity whose exact role remains debated among scholars. He is most often associated with the sun, although some interpretations connect him to the moon. The Primary Chronicle mentions Hors alongside other deities like Perun and Dazhbog, indicating his importance in the pre-Christian Slavic pantheon. His name's etymology is uncertain, with proposed connections to Iranian words for 'sun' or 'radiant.' Unlike some Slavic deities with well-defined narratives, Hors lacks extensive mythology, making him somewhat enigmatic. He is believed to have been worshipped across Eastern Slavic territories, and his presence in historical texts provides valuable insight into the religious beliefs of the time. Some researchers link him to solar horses, suggesting a symbolic connection to the sun's daily journey across the sky. While his attributes are less clear than those of other Slavic gods, Hors represents a significant figure in understanding the complex and varied nature of Slavic paganism. He may have been responsible for ensuring the sun's daily passage, bringing light and warmth to the world. The relative scarcity of information about Hors highlights the challenges in reconstructing Slavic mythology from fragmented historical sources. He likely held a prominent position in the pantheon, judging from his mention alongside more well-documented deities. His association with the sun, whether direct or symbolic, places him among the vital forces that governed the natural world in Slavic belief systems.
subtitle: The Enigmatic Slavic Sun God

keyMyths.title: Mention in the Primary Chronicle
keyMyths.description: The Primary Chronicle mentions Hors alongside other major Slavic deities, indicating his significance in the pantheon before the conversion to Christianity. This is one of the primary sources of information about him.

keyMyths.title: Etymological Debate
keyMyths.description: The etymology of Hors's name is debated, with some scholars suggesting a connection to Iranian words for 'sun' or 'radiant'. This links him linguistically to solar concepts.

keyMyths.title: Solar Horses
keyMyths.description: Some interpretations associate Hors with solar horses, suggesting a symbolic connection to the sun's daily journey across the sky. This reinforces his potential solar role.

sources.author: Helmold of Bosau
